findings.title: Any verified isolation pool can arbitrarily lock or unlock unrelated positions

best_agent_match.title: AaveHub and IsolationPool can bypass all security checks

rejected_candidates.title: Master can drain excess tokens from any pool via skim function
rejected_candidates.reason: `skim()` only transfers unaccounted excess to the fixed `master` address and does not let an external attacker redirect accounted pool funds; this is an intentional admin rescue/design path, not an exploit.

rejected_candidates.title: No emergency stop mechanism - unfixable critical bugs lead to permanent loss
rejected_candidates.reason: Lack of a pause switch is a governance/design choice, not a concrete vulnerability in the implemented logic.

rejected_candidates.title: Approval front-running vulnerability - no increaseAllowance function
rejected_candidates.reason: The described attack is not supported by this custom allowance model; a third party cannot front-run to grant themselves approval.

rejected_candidates.title: Oracle price manipulation leading to incorrect liquidations
rejected_candidates.reason: This is a generic oracle-risk claim without source-level evidence that the integrated oracle logic here is manipulable or missing required freshness protections.

rejected_candidates.title: AaveHub and IsolationPool can bypass all security checks
rejected_candidates.reason: These are explicitly privileged/trusted roles by design. The report does not show an untrusted path that grants this bypass capability.

rejected_candidates.title: Share price manipulation via large deposits/withdrawals
rejected_candidates.reason: No concrete exploit path is shown beyond normal share-accounting behavior; the cited code does not support a standalone manipulation bug.

rejected_candidates.title: Rounding up in paybackAmount causes users to overpay
rejected_candidates.reason: Ceiling division in `paybackAmount()` is expected when repaying an exact number of borrow shares and does not by itself create an exploitable loss-of-funds bug.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing zero address validation in setSecurity allows setting zero address
rejected_candidates.reason: `setSecurity()` is one-time, `onlyMaster`, and a zero or non-contract address would fail initialization rather than silently disable security for attackers.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing validation on collateral factor upper bound in createPool
rejected_candidates.reason: This is an admin-only misconfiguration risk, not an externally exploitable vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Position NFT ID not validated - possible array out of bounds
rejected_candidates.reason: The cited storage uses mappings, not arrays, and ownership-sensitive flows are checked through `WISE_SECURITY`; no out-of-bounds condition is supported.

rejected_candidates.title: Flash loan attack vector on liquidation rewards
rejected_candidates.reason: This is another generic oracle-manipulation claim without code-specific evidence that liquidation pricing can be flash-loan manipulated here.

rejected_candidates.title: Unchecked return value in receive function
rejected_candidates.reason: `receive()` forwards ETH via `_sendValue()`, which explicitly reverts on failure; it does not fail silently.

rejected_candidates.title: Potential integer overflow in fee share calculation
rejected_candidates.reason: Solidity 0.8.x checked arithmetic would revert on overflow, so this is not a silent overflow vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: No timelock on master actions - immediate effect
rejected_candidates.reason: Absence of a timelock is a governance/trust-model issue, not a code-exploitable vulnerability by untrusted actors.
